activation_mode.mechanism: Gemini CLI rule files load unconditionally; no conditional activation syntax documented

auto_memory.mechanism: memory_command: Gemini CLI /memory command saves notes that persist across sessions as rule-like content

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: Gemini CLI reads its own GEMINI.md format; no cross-provider rule file recognition documented

file_imports.mechanism: file_imports extension: Gemini CLI supports including external file content into rule files via import directives

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: hierarchical_context_loading: Gemini CLI loads GEMINI.md files from multiple directory levels with defined precedence

providerExtensions.name: Hierarchical GEMINI.md loading
providerExtensions.description: Gemini CLI loads GEMINI.md files from three tiers in order: (1) global (~/.gemini/GEMINI.md), (2) workspace/environment context files found by searching configured workspace directories and their parent directories, and (3) just-in-time (JIT) context files discovered automatically when a tool accesses a file or directory — scanning that directory and its ancestors up to a trusted root. All found files are concatenated and sent to the model with every prompt. The CLI footer displays the number of loaded context files.

providerExtensions.name: Configurable context file name
providerExtensions.description: The default context filename is GEMINI.md, but users can configure alternative names or a list of names via the context.fileName property in settings.json. For example: {"context": {"fileName": ["AGENTS.md", "CONTEXT.md", "GEMINI.md"]}}. The CLI checks each name in order.

providerExtensions.name: File import directives
providerExtensions.description: GEMINI.md files can import content from other files using the @file.md syntax (e.g., @./components/instructions.md or @../shared/style-guide.md). Both relative and absolute paths are supported. Imported content is inlined before being sent to the model, allowing large rule sets to be modularized across multiple files.

providerExtensions.name: /memory management command
providerExtensions.description: The /memory command provides runtime management of loaded context files: /memory show displays the full concatenated active context; /memory reload forces a re-scan of all GEMINI.md files without restarting; /memory add <text> appends text directly to ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md for persistent in-session memory capture.
